title: erythrostemon exostemma subsp. tampicoanus
intro: Erythrostemon exostemma subsp. tampicoanus, also known as the Tampico bird of paradise, is a plant species native to Mexico. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is used for various health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in various herbal preparations, including tinctures and salves.
